item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.6 No.7-10, 1958
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

sketch_descriptions: Group of multiple men wearing blazers and hats standing and conversing, some colored in purple and red with stripes.
Various smaller sketches of groups of people mingling, including men and women, some with parasols or hats.
Two seated figures on the left, one appearing to be a woman in a hat and the other in a wheelchair or stroller, facing left.
A small sketch of boats or a rowing scene near the water with people on a boat.
Several additional small crowd scenes scattered across the top left and top right edges.
Figures wearing striped boots and blazers, suggesting an outdoors sporting event.
Illustrations capture the social and sporting atmosphere of the Henley Regatta, with emphasis on fashion and crowds.
pages.page_summary: This page illustrates social scenes and fashionable attendees at the Henley Regatta event, highlighting groups of men and women in period attire around the rowing competition.
